req.title: Upload onboarding document — variant 4
req.statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three testable axes: (1) file format allowlist (PDF/PNG/JPG), (2) size limit of 10 MB, and (3) per-user rate limiting. EP partitions valid formats vs. invalid formats and valid sizes vs. oversized files. BVA targets the exact 10 MB boundary (below, at, and above) — only positive and boundary kinds are assigned to BVA tests; the over-limit rejection is re-classified to NEG+EP because it belongs to the invalid equivalence partition, not a boundary exploration. STC covers the upload state machine transitions (unauthenticated -> rejected, authenticated -> accepted) and is assigned kind=positive to reflect the successful terminal state being verified; the unauthenticated leg is an intermediate step within the same state-transition scenario, so the overall test kind is positive (valid transition sequence exercised end-to-end). DT covers rate-limit exhaustion combinations. Together these yield: positive/EP (valid PDF under limit), boundary/BVA (exactly 10 MB PNG), negative/NEG (oversized file -> 413), negative/NEG (disallowed mime -> 415), negative/DT (rate-limit exhaustion -> 429), and positive/STC (state transition unauthenticated then authenticated). Violations fixed: test[2] changed from heuristic=BVA to heuristic=NEG; test[5] changed from kind=negative to kind=positive.

tests.name: test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_returns_200_and_queues_scan
preconditions: User is authenticated and holds a valid session token
File is a valid PDF smaller than 10 MB
actions: Construct a 1 MB in-memory PDF byte stream
POST the file to /documents/upload with Content-Type multipart/form-data
expected: Response body contains a document_id field
Response body contains virus_scan_status equal to 'queued'
trace: R-WEB-080: valid PDF <= 10 MB must be stored and virus scan queued
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"
ENDPOINT = "/documents/upload"

def test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_returns_200_and_queues_scan():
    headers = <auth_headers>(user_id="employee_01")
    pdf_bytes = <make_file_bytes>(size_bytes=1 * 1024 * 1024, mime="application/pdf")
    files = {"file": ("onboarding.pdf", pdf_bytes, "application/pdf")}
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=headers, files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, "document_id missing from response"
    assert body.get("virus_scan_status") == "queued", "Virus scan not queued"

tests.name: test_upload_file_exactly_10mb_is_accepted
preconditions: File size is exactly 10 485 760 bytes (10 MiB)
actions: Construct an in-memory PNG byte stream of exactly 10 485 760 bytes
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 status code (boundary value is inclusive)
Response body contains virus_scan_status equal to 'queued'
trace: R-WEB-080: files up to 10 MB must be accepted; exactly 10 MB is the inclusive upper boundary
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"
ENDPOINT = "/documents/upload"
TEN_MB = 10 * 1024 * 1024

def test_upload_file_exactly_10mb_is_accepted():
    headers = <auth_headers>(user_id="employee_02")
    png_bytes = <make_file_bytes>(size_bytes=TEN_MB, mime="image/png")
    files = {"file": ("doc.png", png_bytes, "image/png")}
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=headers, files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"10 MB file rejected: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body
    assert body.get("virus_scan_status") == "queued"

tests.name: test_upload_file_one_byte_over_10mb_returns_413
preconditions: File size is 10 485 761 bytes (10 MiB + 1 byte)
actions: Construct an in-memory JPEG byte stream of 10 485 761 bytes
expected: Response body indicates payload too large
trace: R-WEB-080: files > 10 MB must be rejected with HTTP 413
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"
ENDPOINT = "/documents/upload"
OVER_LIMIT = 10 * 1024 * 1024 + 1

def test_upload_file_one_byte_over_10mb_returns_413():
    headers = <auth_headers>(user_id="employee_03")
    jpeg_bytes = <make_file_bytes>(size_bytes=OVER_LIMIT, mime="image/jpeg")
    files = {"file": ("big.jpg", jpeg_bytes, "image/jpeg")}
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=headers, files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 413, f"Expected 413, got {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415
preconditions: File MIME type is application/zip (not in allow-list)
File size is within the 10 MB limit
actions: Construct a 512 KB in-memory byte stream with MIME type application/zip
POST the file to /documents/upload with Content-Type multipart/form-data
expected: Response body indicates unsupported media type
trace: R-WEB-080: MIME types outside PDF/PNG/JPG allowlist must be rejected with HTTP 415
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"
ENDPOINT = "/documents/upload"

def test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415():
    headers = <auth_headers>(user_id="employee_04")
    zip_bytes = <make_file_bytes>(size_bytes=512 * 1024, mime="application/zip")
    files = {"file": ("archive.zip", zip_bytes, "application/zip")}
    resp = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=headers, files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 415, f"Expected 415, got {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_rate_limit_exceeded_returns_429_after_threshold
preconditions: Rate-limit threshold N is known and configured (default assumed: 5 per minute)
Valid PDF files under 10 MB are used for each request
actions: Send N valid upload requests in rapid succession for the same user
Capture HTTP response of the N+1 request
expected: Requests 1 through N return HTTP 200 or 201
Request N+1 returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
Response body or headers include Retry-After information
trace: R-WEB-080: operation must be rate-limited per user; excess requests must be rejected
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"
ENDPOINT = "/documents/upload"
RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD = <get_rate_limit_threshold>()

def test_rate_limit_exceeded_returns_429_after_threshold():
    headers = <auth_headers>(user_id="employee_ratelimit")
    pdf_bytes = <make_file_bytes>(size_bytes=512 * 1024, mime="application/pdf")
    for i in range(RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD):
        files = {"file": (f"doc_{i}.pdf", pdf_bytes, "application/pdf")}
        r = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=headers, files=files)
        assert r.status_code in (200, 201), f"Request {i+1} failed early: {r.status_code}"
    files = {"file": ("extra.pdf", pdf_bytes, "application/pdf")}
    r_over = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=headers, files=files)
    assert r_over.status_code == 429, f"Expected 429, got {r_over.status_code}"
    assert "Retry-After" in r_over.headers or "retry_after" in r_over.json()

tests.name: test_upload_state_transition_unauthenticated_then_authenticated
preconditions: No session token is present for the initial request
A valid session token is available for the second request
File is a valid JPG under 10 MB
actions: POST a valid JPG file to /documents/upload WITHOUT an auth token
Assert the response is a rejection (HTTP 401 or 403) confirming the unauthenticated state blocks the transition
POST the same file WITH a valid auth token
Assert the response is HTTP 200 or 201 confirming the authenticated state permits the upload transition
expected: First request returns HTTP 401 or 403 (unauthenticated state rejects transition)
Second request returns HTTP 200 or 201 (authenticated state completes transition)
Second response body contains document_id and virus_scan_status equal to 'queued'
trace: R-WEB-080: state machine must reject unauthenticated uploads and accept authenticated uploads; both transitions must be exercised
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"
ENDPOINT = "/documents/upload"

def test_upload_state_transition_unauthenticated_then_authenticated():
    jpg_bytes = <make_file_bytes>(size_bytes=512 * 1024, mime="image/jpeg")
    files = {"file": ("onboarding.jpg", jpg_bytes, "image/jpeg")}
    resp_unauth = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, files=files)
    assert resp_unauth.status_code in (401, 403), f"Expected 401/403 for unauthenticated, got {resp_unauth.status_code}"
    headers = <auth_headers>(user_id="employee_stc")
    files = {"file": ("onboarding.jpg", jpg_bytes, "image/jpeg")}
    resp_auth = requests.post(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=headers, files=files)
    assert resp_auth.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 for authenticated, got {resp_auth.status_code}"
    body = resp_auth.json()
    assert "document_id" in body
    assert body.get("virus_scan_status") == "queued"
